title: WKD Auth Access Policies
description: Typed descriptors for endpoint access policies. Consumed by EndpointDescriptor.access in every domain schema. Mirrors the Express middleware chain defined in auth.middleware.js.

AccessPolicy.description: Union of all valid access policy types. Use oneOf discriminated by the 'type' property.

Public.description: No authentication required — endpoint is openly accessible without a JWT. Examples: login, register, health check.

Private.description: JWT Bearer token required. The 'protect' Express middleware validates the token and attaches req.user. Any authenticated role may access.

middleware.description: Express middleware chain applied in route definition order

RoleRestricted.description: JWT required (protect) plus an explicit role check (authorizeRoles). Endpoint is only accessible to users whose role is in the 'roles' list.

roles.description: One or more roles permitted — controllers may further restrict based on resource ownership

PublicTokenized.description: No JWT required, but access is scoped by a short-lived opaque share token embedded in the URL path. Used for customer-facing share links (quotation PDF, invoice approval). Token is validated inside the controller, not in middleware.

tokenParam.description: Name of the Express path parameter holding the share token

notes.description: Security notes — token scope, expiry policy, permitted actions

PolicyMatrix.description: Role × Resource access summary. Informational — not used for runtime enforcement. Truth is in middleware chains.

superAdmin.description: Full access to all domains and admin endpoints

businessAdministrator.description: Full CRUD on all resources; no platform admin functions

fieldServiceAgent.description: Read own profile; update assigned service calls; self-dispatch where enabled

customer.description: Respond to public share token links only; no authenticated API access in current version
